title: Life Support and Eva
wikitext:
[]: Life Support and Eva             Quote Post by Formless One » Thu Jul 17, 2008 8:10 pm The Evangelion units are designed to keep their pilots alive, not just in combat conditions, but for extended periods of time if necessary as seen when unit 01 is trapped in Lelliel's pocket dimension. If a similar situation came up when power concerns are not an issue, such as if the Eva has an S^2 engine, How much time would the pilot have? This was originally going to be part of a fanfic I was going to write in which Unit 04 escapes from the Sea of Dirac with the pilot alive, but I don't know the maximum amount of time I could allow for such an event to take place. Before anyone jumps on me for suggesting that Unit 04 survived or that it probably didn't have a pilot yet, that is for another time. Right now I'm just curious; in such a situation, how long would they have?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 17, 2008 8:14 pm Depends on exactly how LCL filtration (and reoxygenation, for that matter) works and how long it can be maintained independent of Nerv's management. And there's the longer-term problems of the pilot dehydrating and/or starving to death.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Thu Jul 17, 2008 9:43 pm Hmmm... that makes this question infinitely more morbid then. How long would it take for them to die? This is all out of curiosity, remember. The story is on hold for now until I get more info.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 17, 2008 10:07 pm These details weren't worked out in the show, so you basically have to "make shit up".

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Jul 17, 2008 10:11 pm Reichu wrote: These details weren't worked out in the show, so you basically get to "make shit up".

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Thu Jul 17, 2008 10:29 pm do the plugsuits have systems for waste 'evacuation?' if so i could imagine an pilot could live within an s2 equipped eva nearly indefinitely.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Jul 17, 2008 10:44 pm slothen wrote: do the plugsuits have systems for waste 'evacuation?' if so i could imagine an pilot could live within an s2 equipped eva nearly indefinitely.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 17, 2008 10:51 pm With a baby, the fetus' accessory organs and the woman's body are maintaining the internal environment and taking care of waste and all that. The entry plug is entirely artificial and its internal environment is maintained by technological systems. Is simply having a consistent power feed going to be enough to take care of everything? Without any form of external maintenance, we're talking an entirely closed system here...

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Thu Jul 17, 2008 10:52 pm Make Shit Up... if I have to take this approach, I at least want to get some things clear. I vaguely remember the show mentioning that the LCL provided the pilot essential nutrients as well as O2 in one of the early episodes when describing the entry plug. I could be wrong though, but if this rings a bell to anyone could you confirm? with a quote, preferably. I want my fanwank to be informed and make sense so its easier for me to take in. Everyone else has to suffer through it regardless.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 17, 2008 11:04 pm Don't remember anything about nutrients. Only result for "nutrients" in my Eva files are in NGE glossaries drawing comparison between the umbilical cable and the umbilical cord. However... Episode 01 wrote: Ibuki (RADIO - OFF): Filling the entry plug. Shinji: What is this stuff? RITSUKO: Don't worry. Once your lungs are filled with LCL, your blood will be oxygenated directly. You'll get used to it. Shinji: I feel nauseous.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Thu Jul 17, 2008 11:17 pm Hmmmmm... I didn't remember Shinji mentioning being hungry. But the rest seem to be there to indicate the dwindling energy supply. The temperature control, the purification system... these probably wouldn't be an issue in an S^2 powered Eva. They will eventually wear out due to maintenance concerns, but that could take a long time to happen, and temperature isn't so critical to survival anyway compared to food, water, and air. In keeping with the morbid questions, which problem should kill first? Which ones are not an issue? I want to get to the bottom of this if possible. I haven't given up on that story yet...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jul 18, 2008 12:38 am dehydration shouldn't be a problem, but even if the eva does make more lcl and continually reoxygenated I would expect malnutrition to eventually do a pilot in, and none of them have the fat reserves to last very long.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jul 18, 2008 2:07 am NemZ wrote: dehydration shouldn't be a problem

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jul 18, 2008 2:12 am Reichu wrote: Why, because they have a fluid providing oxygen to their lungs?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jul 18, 2008 2:21 am We don't know much about the chemical properties of "straight LCL", so it's at least possible that drinking it would not rehydrate you. I.e., even though it contains water, the water would not be "accessible" in a way analogous to salt water. Just something to keep in mind.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Fri Jul 18, 2008 4:28 am An easier solution would be to say time slows down in the dirac sea. Or claim entry plugs can freeze you.

[]:             Quote Post by p06 » Fri Jul 18, 2008 5:19 am LCL is supposed to be the primordial soup of life, i'd expect the pilot to be able to survive inside indefinitely provided there is an energy source. In the worst scenario if it is somehow unable to directly sustain the biology of the pilot, it can just dissolve the pilot and have its soul floating around? Speaking of the episode, i had always thought that the sea of dirac was the result of a failed merge between the S2 engine and unit 04. Analogous to some sort of organ rejection in organ transplants.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jul 18, 2008 6:33 am p06 wrote: Speaking of the episode, i had always thought that the sea of dirac was the result of a failed merge between the S2 engine and unit 04. Analogous to some sort of organ rejection in organ transplants.

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Fri Jul 18, 2008 8:46 am Monk Ed wrote: According to that episode, the real reason was never found out, and the possibilities were astronomically numerous. >32,768

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jul 18, 2008 10:53 am master_lloyd wrote: An easier solution would be to say time slows down in the dirac sea.

If a similar situation came up when power concerns are not an issue, such as if the Eva has an S^2 engine, How much time would the pilot have? This
